bead.title: metric: FEAT-014/FEAT-016 cross-reference edits
bead.description: Cross-reference FEAT-014 (token awareness) and FEAT-016 (process metrics) with the new MET artifact convention.
bead.acceptance: 1. Both FEATs have explicit cross-refs to MET artifacts. 2. ddx doc audit clean.

FEAT-014 and FEAT-016 already contain explicit MET wiki links, so no cross-reference edit was necessary in the named scope. Evidence: docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-014-token-awareness.md:434-437 links [[MET-001]] and [[MET-002]], and docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-016-process-metrics.md:113-116 links [[MET-002]]. The remaining acceptance gate, "ddx doc audit clean", is blocked by preexisting repo-wide integrity issues outside this bead's scope: `ddx doc audit` reports 86 issues total, including 84 duplicate_id entries, one cycle involving FEAT-002 -> FEAT-008 -> FEAT-013 -> FEAT-014 -> FEAT-016 -> FEAT-020 -> MET-002 -> SD-013 -> SD-019 -> FEAT-002, and one missing dependency for helix.workflow.principles. Fixing that graph drift requires broader doc-graph cleanup beyond this targeted cross-reference bead.

FEAT-014 and FEAT-016 already contain explicit MET cross-references (`[[MET-001]]` and `[[MET-002]]`) in their Dependencies sections, so the requested content change is already present in the worktree. The blocking issue is the required `ddx doc audit --json --exit-zero`, which is not clean before any edits and reports pre-existing unrelated graph drift: a cycle `FEAT-002 -> FEAT-008 -> FEAT-013 -> FEAT-014 -> FEAT-016 -> FEAT-020 -> MET-002 -> SD-013 -> SD-019 -> FEAT-002`, many duplicate document IDs under `.agents/skills/docs/`, and one missing dependency on `helix.workflow`. Fixing that baseline drift is outside the scope of this bead.
